notes: Chunk 75 DONE (Template E, Tier B, 25 new routes, 1 block) + Blog Day 28. Closed out the Turkey cluster opened in chunk 74: 5 remaining Turkey-to-X routes built (Uruguay, Jamaica, Dominican Republic, Panama, Uganda), all reusing verified export/import blocks from sibling files plus fresh web-verified airline facts (Turkish Airlines' new July 2025 Istanbul-Entebbe direct service, the existing Istanbul-Panama City non-stop, and confirmed no non-stop options into Montevideo, Santo Domingo or Kingston). A gap-analysis agent then swept all 6,296 existing route slugs against the full 90-country network and found exactly 19 remaining Tier B stragglers plus one thematically-relevant addition: 9 mid-tier countries missing their inbound-to-United-States route (Bangladesh, Cambodia, Ethiopia, Luxembourg, Mauritius, Morocco, Myanmar, Slovakia, Zimbabwe; CDC high-risk status confirmed per-country via CDC's own published list, with Bangladesh/Cambodia/Ethiopia/Morocco/Myanmar/Zimbabwe requiring the FAVN titre test and Luxembourg/Mauritius/Slovakia not), an APAC-to-LatAm/Indonesia sub-cluster (Philippines and Malaysia to Brazil/Mexico/Indonesia, South Korea to Indonesia), Greece-to-India, Denmark-to-Brazil/Mexico, and Georgia-to-Armenia. Genuine differentiating facts verified via web search and used on-page: Ethiopian Airlines' non-stop Addis Ababa-Washington Dulles service (ET501, also the connecting leg for Zimbabwe-origin pets); Royal Air Maroc's non-stop Casablanca-JFK service despite Morocco's CDC high-risk status; Cargolux's Luxembourg-US freight network, correctly framed as not the typical individual-pet booking path; KLM's non-stop Amsterdam-Mexico City service used as the through-leg for Denmark-origin pets; the genuine short direct flights Kuala Lumpur-Jakarta and Seoul-Jakarta, both still carrying Indonesia's full titre-test-plus-quarantine process despite the short hop; and Georgia-Armenia's open border and direct Tbilisi-Yerevan flights, a deliberate contrast with Armenia's closed border and absent diplomatic relations with Turkey covered in chunk 74. All 25 files QA-scanned clean (zero em dashes, zero banned vocabulary, zero curly quotes, correct Marcus Webb persona, all internal links verified against actual on-disk target files with zero broken sideways/upward links). Blog Day 28: defra-approved-pet-transport.md (new article, Marcus Webb, approximately 2,500 words), covering DEFRA's Type 1 (over 65km, up to 8 hours) versus Type 2 (over 8 hours) transporter authorisation, how to verify a transporter via the published APHA WIT register or a direct call to the Welfare in Transport team, and how DEFRA authorisation relates to but does not replace IPATA membership, IATA Cargo Agent accreditation and Companies House registration for international moves. Routes 6,296 -> 6,321, blog 430 -> 431, pages 7,100 -> 7,126. Chunk 76 (Template A, Tier B) is next.

counts.note: total_site_pages counts content .md source files. The full deployed total (including Hugo taxonomy and list pages) is verified from sitemap.xml after a build.

counts_method: Reconciled from disk by verify_build_state.py. Never hand-edit count fields; run 'python verify_build_state.py --write' instead.
